title: Early Exploration
description: Jobs works at Atari as a technician and travels to India in search of spiritual enlightenment, experiences that would deeply influence his design philosophy.

title: Founding Apple
description: Apple Computer is founded by Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak in the Jobs family garage, starting a revolution in personal computing with the Apple I.

title: The Macintosh
description: Launch of the first mass-market personal computer with a graphical user interface, introduced by the iconic '1984' commercial during the Super Bowl.

title: The Departure
description: After a power struggle with CEO John Sculley, Jobs leaves Apple and founds NeXT, while also acquiring the Computer Graphics Division of Lucasfilm, which becomes Pixar.

title: The Return
description: Jobs returns to Apple as iCEO after Apple acquires NeXT. He begins the 'Think Different' campaign and initiates one of the greatest turnarounds in business history.

title: The iPod
description: Apple revolutionizes the music industry with '1,000 songs in your pocket,' changing how we consume media and setting the stage for the digital music era.

title: The iPhone
description: Jobs unveils the iPhone, a device that reinvents the phone, the internet, and mobile computing, fundamentally changing the world's digital landscape.

title: Legacy
description: Steve Jobs passes away, leaving a legacy of innovation, design excellence, and a challenge to 'stay hungry, stay foolish' for generations to come.
